steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The opening is a participial phrase (“Woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted...”). The blank requires a subject + verb to form an independent clause, followed by predicate adjectives (“so lush and tactilely inviting...”).

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
A participial phrase at the beginning of a sentence must modify the subject of the independent clause that follows.

Choice A places the tapestries as subject (“the topological tapestries of Argentine textile artist Alexandra Kehayoglou are”), so the participial phrase modifies them.

Choices B, C, and D all place the artist as the main subject, causing the participial phrase to dangle—the artist wasn't woven or hand tufted.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
